content:
First place the rectangle so that  
A=(0,0), B=(5,0), D=(0,12), C=(5,12).  
Since E is 1 from the x‐axis x=0 and 12 from the y‐axis y=0, and ∆ABE must be acute, one checks that  
E=(1,−12).  

A line through E meets AB (y=0) at F=(t,0) and CD (y=12) at G=(2t−1,12).  One finds by integration (or by splitting into trapezoids) that the part of the rectangle “to the left’’ of EG has area  
18t−6,  
and setting 18t−6=30 (half of 60) gives t=2.  Hence  
F=(2,0), G=(3,12),  
and  
FG²=(3−2)²+(12−0)²=1+144=145.  

Answer: \boxed{145}.

First place the rectangle so that  
A=(0,0), B=(5,0), D=(0,12), C=(5,12).  
Since E is 1 from the x‐axis x=0 and 12 from the y‐axis y=0, and ∆ABE must be acute, one checks that  
E=(1,−12).  

A line through E meets AB (y=0) at F=(t,0) and CD (y=12) at G=(2t−1,12).  One finds by integration (or by splitting into trapezoids) that the part of the rectangle “to the left’’ of EG has area  
18t−6,  
and setting 18t−6=30 (half of 60) gives t=2.  Hence  
F=(2,0), G=(3,12),  
and  
FG²=(3−2)²+(12−0)²=1+144=145.  

Answer: \boxed{145}.
